security_trigger_reason: F-0029 implements Telegram egress with bot token handling, operator-only recipient admission, outbound Bot API transport, durable DB evidence, retry/idempotency behavior, terminal timeout behavior, no automatic resend of ambiguous sending rows, and fake Bot API smoke surfaces.

evidence: apps/core/src/actions/tool-gateway.ts:1156 rejects caller-supplied recipient fields via strict telegram.sendMessage parameters before transport
apps/core/src/actions/tool-gateway.ts:1186 binds egress to an existing Telegram stimulus whose chat id matches the configured operator
apps/core/src/actions/tool-gateway.ts:1231 records durable outbox intent before send; :1290 sends stored outbox text, not replay caller text
apps/core/src/actions/tool-gateway.ts:1313 treats telegram_api_timeout as terminal failure; :1345 uses an AbortSignal timeout
packages/db/src/telegram-egress.ts:295 claims only pending/retry_scheduled rows; :380 retries only pending/retry_scheduled rows, not ambiguous sending rows
infra/docker/fake-telegram-api/server.py:93 implements fake sendMessage and :63 exposes sentMessages for smoke observation
infra/docker/deployment-cell.smoke.ts:2017 exercises Telegram overlay and :2106 asserts one sent fake Bot API message to chat 12345

Focused verification: pnpm test -- packages/contracts/test/telegram-egress.contract.test.ts packages/db/test/telegram-egress-store.integration.test.ts apps/core/test/actions/telegram-egress-tool.contract.test.ts apps/core/test/runtime/telegram-egress-retry.contract.test.ts (14 pass, 0 fail)

notes: PASS: reviewed current uncommitted F-0029 implementation after final retry/idempotency fixes.
